pages.raw_text:
The Spokane County Referen-
dunn league will meet tonight at
Apollo hall. G. r. Case will prob
ably be one of the speakers. The
officers of the league invite all
those interested in carrying on the
campaign for the adoption of the
initiative and referendum in this
state to attend. The league was
organized over a year ago and has
been of great assistance in spread
ing the gospel of the peoples rule
throughout the entire state.


Officers for the coming year will
be elected, on whom will, to a
great extent, devolye the conduct
of the coming campaign in this
county and section of the state
The league is nonpartisan and II is
the intention to keep it so.


I'VE have found the voters al
most to man favor the initiative
and referendum" said an officer OF
the league today. Occasionally
we find someone opposed to the
law, but upon investigation it Is
found that their opposition arises
from business interests. We have
every reason to believe that the
amendment to the state constitu
ton will pass the next legislature
and we propose to do everything
possible to see that it does.


The initiative and referendum
amendment to the city charter
should be made effective by reduc
ing the number of signers to a pc
tition from 80 per cent to 10 anG
Is per cent, the same as obtains il
| Oregon. The action OF Baldwin
Funk and Gray and other members
of the council in mutilating the
amendment by making the percen
tage 50 high, tied our hands at tn,
last city election. but we hope tC
be able to show these gentlemen
the error of their way and secure
changes which will make the Law
| effective in this city"
